meta.setting: vertical mine shaft drop at a hard-rock claim outside French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in mine hazard and rescue pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining and extractive labor pressure in the Whiskeytown area.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining-era settlement material and gold-rush context.

image_prompt.subject: a cribbed mine shaft collar with rope ladder, windlass frame, frayed rope, lantern light, loose rock, and miners arguing over a rescue
image_prompt.scene: dusty late afternoon at a claim; one miner kneels by rope marks while another holds a lamp over the dark drop, no readable signs or labels
image_prompt.composition: square danger scene looking across the shaft mouth with the black drop centered, workers and tools arranged around the collar
image_prompt.camera: low over-the-shoulder view from the shaft edge, close enough to read rope fibers and cribbing
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the black drop feel dangerous without hiding the useful evidence around it.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine mine hazard scene
render_traits: slightly simplified miners, shaft collar, windlass, rope, lamp, and rock piles
modest texture resolution on timber, rope, iron, dust, cloth, rock, and soot
hard ambient occlusion under cribbing, workers, windlass, tools, and shaft lip
warm lantern light against harsh dusty exterior shade
believable 1899 mining tools, clothes, and claim-site clutter
playable rescue and evidence composition with clear vertical risk
style_modifiers.palette: timber brown, shaft black, rope tan, dust ochre, iron gray, lantern amber, rock blue-gray, and work-cloth brown.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable notices, crate labels, chalk marks, initials, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The shaft collar, black drop, rope ladder, windlass, miners, loose rock, and rescue tension are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to 1899 hard-rock mining.
The image supports rescue, evidence, labor, hazard, and hidden-cache scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
